topic: Robust evaluation and mitigation of hallucinations in multimodal retrieval-augmented generation across domains

papers.title: Mitigating Visual Hallucinations in Multimodal Systems through Retrieval-Augmented Reliability-Aware Inference
papers.abstract: A retrieval-augmented reliability-aware inference framework uses external visual evidence, similarity strength, class-support agreement, evidence margin, and entropy-based uncertainty to decide whether to answer, answer cautiously, or abstain.

papers.title: Ragas: Automated Evaluation of Retrieval Augmented Generation
papers.abstract: Ragas provides a reference-free suite of metrics for retrieval, context, and generation quality in retrieval-augmented generation pipelines.

research_gaps.title: Generalization of reliability indicators across visual domains
research_gaps.description: Reliability signals derived from a single visual benchmark may not transfer to medical, geospatial, or culturally specific visual domains.

novel_directions.title: Cross-domain reliability benchmark for multimodal RAG
novel_directions.description: Evaluate calibrated abstention and reliability indicators across multiple visual domains with common retrieval corpora and fixed base models.

feasibility.estimated_cost: Moderate compute and curation effort for multiple visual domains.
feasibility.industry_viability: Applicable to AI safety, healthcare, and content-moderation systems.
